title: Split compatibility baseline gates from latest ecosystem probes
outOfScope: Changing stack implementations or installer presets
Changing the release version or publishing a tag
Guaranteeing compatibility with every future ecosystem version

sources.reason: Current compatibility matrix mixes fixed Python lanes with stable/latest ecosystem probes and makes every lane release-blocking.
Workflow regression expectations and compatibility gate contract.
Review recommends compatibility-baseline fixed tools as blocking and compatibility-latest as warning-only exploration.

riskAssessment.reason: A baseline/latest split must preserve deterministic release evidence while making ecosystem drift visible without turning latest probes into release blockers.

executionDecision.reason: The fixed baseline lanes, warning-only latest lanes, and release gate ownership are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review that only fixed baseline lanes feed compatibility-gate and latest probe failures remain visible without blocking release.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: compatibility-gate blocks on the fixed Python matrix and shellcheck baseline only.
Stable/latest real-stack, extended-stack, and mobile probes remain executable but do not block compatibility-gate when they drift.
A named latest-probe summary job exposes the experimental lane outcomes, and tests verify the baseline/latest dependency split.
Documentation states that baseline is release evidence while latest is exploratory and may warn.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.
Release-blocking evidence must use fixed tool versions; latest probes must not silently become release blockers.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: fixed baseline lanes pass and compatibility-gate succeeds
latest probe drifts while baseline remains green
baseline lane fails and compatibility-gate blocks
latest probe outcomes are visible in a non-blocking summary

intent.problem: The compatibility workflow currently treats fixed release checks and moving stable/latest ecosystem probes as one blocking gate, so an expected latest-tool drift can block release while the evidence model remains unclear.
constraints: Keep fixed Python and shellcheck validation release-blocking.
Keep all existing stack probes executable.
Do not claim latest probes are reproducible release evidence.
Do not publish a new version in this Work Item.
intent.rationale: Separate deterministic baseline evidence from exploratory ecosystem detection so release decisions remain reproducible without hiding compatibility drift.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed execution of the planned remediation loop, including CI workflow updates.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
